steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Distribute 19 across both terms inside the parentheses: 19(x^2 - 8) = 19 \cdot x^2 - 19 \cdot 8.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The final check is to match the computed result to the answer choices. The value or equation found above matches $19x^2 - 152$. The other choices do not satisfy the same condition from the question, so D is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — $19x^2 + 11$: The constant term should be -152 (subtracting), not +11. This confuses -8 with -(19-8) = -11 and flips the sign. ✗
  • Choice B — $19x^2 - 8$: Fails to multiply the 8 by 19. This treats the expression as 19x^2 + (-8) without distributing the 19. ✗
  • Choice C — $19x^2 - 27$: Appears to compute 19 - 8 = 11 then subtract differently — no valid distribution yields -27 here. ✗
  • Choice D — $19x^2 - 152$: 19 × x^2 = 19x^2 and 19 × 8 = 152, so the result is 19x^2 - 152. ✓
That is the correct answer.
